name: N.I.C.E.
description_short: N.I.C.E. Federal Credit Union is a small federal credit union in Geneva, IL serving government employees with $2.76M in assets and 357 members since 1983.
description_long: N.I.C.E. Federal Credit Union was established in 1983 and is headquartered in Geneva, Illinois. As a federally-chartered credit union, it operates under the regulatory oversight of the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA) and maintains NCUA insurance protection for member deposits through the National Credit Union Share Insurance Fund (NCUSIF). The institution has remained relatively modest in size, with approximately 357 members and a single branch location at its main office on E State Street in Geneva. The credit union serves members primarily through a multiple common bond structure, with membership focused on federal, state, and local government employees. They offer core banking services including checking and savings accounts, ATM and debit card programs, and various loan products including unsecured loans and other loan categories. As of their most recent audit on September 1, 2024, the institution reported total assets of $2,756,149, with loan portfolios totaling $1,751,140 and member deposits of $2,561,460. N.I.C.E. Federal Credit Union distinguishes itself as a highly specialized, member-focused institution serving government workers in the Illinois region. With 43 years of operational history, the credit union has maintained continuity of service to its niche membership base. The institution is led by CEO/Manager Carla J Santschi and Chairperson Linda J White. Their focused membership model and long operational history suggest deep community ties within their specific membership segment. As a very small credit union with limited assets and a single branch, N.I.C.E. Federal Credit Union may offer limited technology infrastructure and service breadth compared to larger financial institutions. The institution's modest size means members should verify that all their banking needs can be accommodated, particularly regarding online banking capabilities, mobile services, and ATM network access. Prospective members should confirm membership eligibility through their government employment status before applying.
services: Money market or other deposit accounts
ATM access and debit card programs
pros: Federally chartered and NCUA-insured, providing deposit protection up to standard insurance limits
43-year operational history demonstrates stability and established presence serving government employees
Member-focused credit union structure with multiple common bond serving federal, state, and local government employees
Offers ATM/debit card programs and multiple deposit account types (share drafts and regular shares)
Multiple loan products available including unsecured loans and other loan categories
Local presence in Geneva, IL with direct phone and fax contact for member service
Non-profit credit union structure returns earnings to members rather than external shareholders
cons: Very small institution with only $2.76M in assets limits service capabilities, online banking features, and technology infrastructure compared to larger credit unions
Single branch location restricts physical access for members outside the Geneva, IL area
Limited membership eligibility restricted to government employees, excluding general public
Modest loan portfolio of $1.75M may restrict borrowing capacity or product availability for larger loan requests
Minimal net income of $2,015 in latest reporting period suggests thin operating margins and limited reinvestment capacity
best_for: Federal, state, and local government employees in the Illinois area seeking a community-focused credit union
Members prioritizing membership in a long-established institution with 43 years of operational history
Government workers seeking basic banking services including checking, savings, and loan products in the Geneva, IL region
diagnosis: N.I.C.E. Federal Credit Union is best suited for government employees in the Geneva, IL area who want straightforward banking services from a stable, member-focused institution with deep community roots. The primary caveat is that the institution's very small size and single-branch model means limited access to advanced technology, extensive loan products, or broad service capabilities compared to larger financial institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
